releaseYears.top: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

ledger.t: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
Portrait of a Lady on Fire

calibration.t: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

reviews.text: A man lies his way into the truth. Cinema does the same.
The dead narrator tells the truth. Everyone alive lies.
Second watch. It is somehow sadder when you know.
Saw it in 70mm. Different species of experience.
Komorebi as a way of life.
A film that knows what the back of your head looks like.
Not a war film. An exorcism.
It sneaks up on you like age itself.
Watched it twice in one night. Still lost. Still grateful.
The rug pull is that there was no rug.
I do not know what the Zone is and I think about it daily.
Three and a half hours and not one wasted frame.
Two people rehearsing a heartbreak until it becomes one.
The peach fuzz is the whole class war in one image.

films.title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

because.title: Endless Love
because.why: because you loved The Red Shoes

forYou.title: Blue Velvet

forYou.title: A Bronx Tale
forYou.why: via The Godfather + Do the Right Thing

forYou.title: The Godfather Part III
forYou.why: via The Godfather + Do the Right Thing

courses.title: The grammar — silents & foundations

extra.title: Safety Last!
extra.why: Harold Lloyd on the clock face — comedy as vertigo

courses.desc: Where the language was invented: framing, montage, the close-up, and finally sound. Everything after is dialect.
courses.assignment: Watch the Odessa Steps sequence twice; the second time, count the cuts.

films.title: Sunrise: A Song of Two Humans
films.why: pure visual storytelling — the camera untethered

films.title: Battleship Potemkin
films.why: montage as argument; the Odessa steps built modern editing

films.title: The General
films.why: action choreography and comic geometry, still unmatched

films.title: The Passion of Joan of Arc
films.why: what a face in close-up can carry

films.title: M
films.why: sound arrives — and is immediately used as dread

courses.title: Classical Hollywood
courses.desc: The studio system as a machine for storytelling: coverage, continuity, stars, and the invisible style that still runs everything you stream.
courses.assignment: In Kane, find every cut that jumps years without a title card.

films.title: Citizen Kane
films.why: deep focus, structure, ego — the textbook that is also a film

films.title: Casablanca
films.why: the studio system at maximum efficiency and feeling

films.title: Sunset Boulevard
films.why: Hollywood autopsying itself, narrated by a corpse

films.title: Singin’ in the Rain
films.why: form as joy; the industry laughing at its own sound panic

films.title: Vertigo
films.why: obsession as camera movement — the canon’s dark heart

courses.title: The documentary claim

extra.title: Grizzly Man
extra.why: Herzog arguing with his own footage

courses.desc: What does it mean to point a camera at the real? Truth, staging, memory and the essay film — nonfiction as a form, not a genre.
courses.assignment: After The Thin Blue Line, write down what the re-enactments made you believe, and when.

films.title: Man with a Movie Camera
films.why: the city symphony — editing as pure exhilaration

films.title: Night and Fog
films.why: thirty-two minutes; the heaviest film ever made

films.title: Sans Soleil
films.why: the essay film — memory wearing a travelogue’s clothes

films.title: The Thin Blue Line
films.why: a documentary that overturned a conviction — and invented a style

films.title: Hoop Dreams
films.why: five years, two families — structure found, not written

courses.title: The animated image

extra.title: Waltz with Bashir
extra.why: the animated documentary — memory as war

courses.desc: Animation is not a genre for children; it is cinema freed from physics. Watch what becomes possible when every frame is authored.
courses.assignment: In Spirited Away, track how weight is animated — food, water, a train.

films.title: Spirited Away
films.why: world-building with the logic of dreams and the rules of folklore

films.title: Grave of the Fireflies
films.why: the proof animation can hold the unbearable

films.title: Akira
films.why: scale, motion and detail that live action still cannot buy

films.title: Persepolis
films.why: a memoir in ink — style as voice

films.title: Fantastic Mr. Fox
films.why: stop-motion as personality; texture you can feel

courses.title: Neorealism & the New Wave

extra.title: Umberto D.
extra.why: De Sica at his most unsparing

courses.desc: Cinema leaves the studio, then breaks its own rules on purpose. Non-actors, real streets, jump cuts — the invention of the personal film.
courses.assignment: Count the jump cuts in the first ten minutes of Breathless. Decide which ones you felt.

films.title: Bicycle Thieves
films.why: non-actors, real streets — cinema leaves the studio

films.title: Breathless
films.why: the jump cut as rebellion; rules broken on purpose

films.title: The 400 Blows
films.why: the personal film — and that freeze-frame

films.title: Cléo from 5 to 7
films.why: real time, a woman watched and watching

films.title: La Dolce Vita
films.why: the episodic film; glamour as emptiness

courses.title: The world masters

extra.title: Late Spring
extra.why: Ozu again — the smile that isn’t one

courses.desc: Four national cinemas at their peaks. Watch for how differently a story can breathe — the tatami shot, the weather, the ensemble, the icon.
courses.assignment: In Tokyo Story, note every time the camera moves. It will not take long.

films.title: Tokyo Story
films.why: stillness as devastation; the tatami shot

films.title: Seven Samurai
films.why: ensemble, geography, weather — action with a soul

films.title: The Rules of the Game
films.why: the deep-focus ensemble — everyone lying in the same frame

films.title: Persona
films.why: the film that interrogates its own projector

films.title: Andrei Rublev
films.why: faith, art and mud — the long take as prayer

courses.title: Indian cinema — the parallel canon
courses.desc: Ray’s humanism, Ghatak’s wounds, Guru Dutt’s shadows, and the masala epic — a full national cinema that most syllabi skip. Not this one.
courses.assignment: Watch Pyaasa’s songs as scenes: what does each one do that dialogue couldn’t?

films.title: Pather Panchali
films.why: a whole cinema born in one village

films.title: Pyaasa
films.why: song as soliloquy; light as heartbreak

films.title: The Cloud-Capped Star
films.why: Ghatak — melodrama sharpened into a scream

films.title: Charulata
films.why: Ray’s chamber piece; a marriage in glances

films.title: Sholay
films.why: the masala epic — genre synthesis on the widest possible screen

courses.title: Film noir

extra.title: In a Lonely Place
extra.why: noir where the darkness is the hero

courses.desc: Shadows, fatalism and voiceover: the American style built from German expressionism and post-war dread. Study the lighting before the plot.
courses.assignment: In Double Indemnity, note when you first know it ends badly — the film tells you immediately. Why does it still work?

films.title: Double Indemnity
films.why: the template — desire, insurance, and a voice from the grave

films.title: Out of the Past
films.why: the past as gravity; nobody escapes the flashback

films.title: The Third Man
films.why: tilted frames, a zither, and the best entrance in cinema

films.title: Touch of Evil
films.why: the opening crane shot every film school screens

films.title: Sweet Smell of Success
films.why: dialogue as switchblade; the city at night

courses.title: New Hollywood

extra.title: Five Easy Pieces
extra.why: the chicken-salad scene; drift as drama

courses.desc: The auteurs take the keys to the studio. Moral ambiguity, location shooting, and endings that refuse to comfort.
courses.assignment: Chinatown: mark the moment Gittes stops being ahead of the audience.

films.title: The Godfather
films.why: lighting, patience, succession — commerce perfected into art

films.title: Chinatown
films.why: the screenplay every program still assigns

films.title: Taxi Driver
films.why: subjectivity — the city as a diseased mind

films.title: Apocalypse Now
films.why: production as madness, sound design as war

films.title: Days of Heaven
films.why: magic-hour light; images that replace dialogue

courses.title: Modern world cinema

extra.title: Memories of Murder
extra.why: the procedural that indicts the procedure

courses.desc: The last thirty years of the art house: withheld desire, dissolving fact and fiction, and class warfare staged as architecture.
courses.assignment: In the Mood for Love: count the different appearances of the same corridor.

films.title: In the Mood for Love
films.why: repetition, frames within frames, desire withheld

films.title: Yi Yi
films.why: the family epic at eye level

films.title: Close-Up
films.why: documentary and fiction dissolving into each other

films.title: A Separation
films.why: moral complexity with no villain in the room

films.title: Parasite
films.why: architecture as class; genre as trojan horse

courses.title: Horror & the body
courses.desc: The genre where form is felt physically. Suspense vs. shock, implication vs. display — and why the scariest cut is the one you don’t see.
courses.assignment: Psycho’s shower scene: list what you never actually see.

films.title: Psycho
films.why: the rules of suspense broken over the audience’s head

films.title: Rosemary’s Baby
films.why: dread as domesticity; the horror of being disbelieved

films.title: The Texas Chain Saw Massacre
films.why: texture and sound — almost bloodless, entirely unbearable

films.title: The Shining
films.why: geometry as menace; the Steadicam as ghost

films.title: Don’t Look Now
films.why: grief edited into premonition

courses.title: The Western
courses.desc: America arguing with its own myth. Landscape as morality, and fifty years of the genre revising itself.
courses.assignment: The Searchers: decide whether the last doorway shot forgives Ethan. Defend it.

films.title: The Searchers
films.why: the myth and its rot in the same frame

films.title: High Noon
films.why: real time as moral pressure

films.title: Once Upon a Time in the West
films.why: the genre slowed to opera

films.title: McCabe & Mrs. Miller
films.why: the anti-western — mud, snow and Leonard Cohen

films.title: Unforgiven
films.why: the genre’s own eulogy, delivered by its icon

courses.title: Science fiction & the future
courses.desc: Ideas wearing production design. The genre that lets cinema think out loud about time, machines and what counts as human.
courses.assignment: La Jetée is twenty-eight minutes of still images. Note the one moment that moves.

films.title: Metropolis
films.why: the city of the future, built once and borrowed forever

films.title: La Jetée
films.why: a film of photographs that out-moves most movies

films.title: Solaris
films.why: science fiction as grief — the ocean that answers back

films.title: Alien
films.why: industrial space; horror engineered like a machine

films.title: Blade Runner
films.why: atmosphere as argument — what a world can say

courses.title: Form studies — editing, time, sound
courses.desc: The capstone in technique: point of view, the cut across millennia, sound as testimony, and dream logic that holds.
courses.assignment: Rashomon: write the fourth version of events nobody tells.

films.title: Rashomon
films.why: point of view as epistemology

films.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
films.why: the match cut across four million years

films.title: Come and See
films.why: sound and face — cinema as testimony

films.title: Mulholland Drive
films.why: dream logic held together by feeling, not plot

films.title: There Will Be Blood
films.why: performance, score and landscape as one organism

courses.title: Slow cinema & the long take
courses.desc: Duration as a tool. What happens to attention when the cut refuses to come — boredom, then something on the far side of it.
courses.assignment: Jeanne Dielman: notice the exact moment routine becomes suspense.

films.title: Jeanne Dielman, 23, quai du Commerce, 1080 Bruxelles
films.why: three days of routine — then the greatest ellipsis in cinema

films.title: Playtime
films.why: the frame so wide you choose your own film

films.title: Stalker
films.why: the long take as pilgrimage

films.title: Werckmeister Harmonies
films.why: thirty-nine shots; a whale and a riot

films.title: A Brighter Summer Day
films.why: four hours earned — history at the scale of a schoolyard

courses.title: Comedy as precision
courses.desc: The hardest form taught last. Timing, framing and escalation — comedy is editing you can hear the audience grade.
courses.assignment: Some Like It Hot: clock the last line. Nothing after it — that’s the lesson.

films.title: Duck Soup
films.why: anarchy with perfect timing; the mirror scene

films.title: Some Like It Hot
films.why: structure so tight the jokes feel inevitable

films.title: Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb
films.why: the end of the world, played straight

films.title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail
films.why: budget as running gag; parody with scholarship

films.title: Toni Erdmann
films.why: the modern comedy of embarrassment, held one beat too long — on purpose

courses.title: The thesis — cinema about cinema

extra.title: Sullivan’s Travels
extra.why: a comedy about why comedy matters

courses.desc: Graduation: films that turn the camera on the act of making films. If the medium can examine itself, so can you.
courses.assignment: 8½: separate the scenes that happen from the ones Guido imagines. Give up. That’s the point.

films.title: 8½
films.why: the artist’s block as carnival — the self-portrait every director steals from

films.title: Day for Night
films.why: the set as family; the trouble as the movie

films.title: Cinema Paradiso
films.why: projection as memory; the kiss reel

films.title: Barton Fink
films.why: the writer’s room as inferno

films.title: Adaptation.
films.why: the screenplay eating itself and surviving

courses.title: First features — the debut
courses.desc: What a first film can announce. Five debuts where the voice arrived already whole — study what each director never had to learn.
courses.assignment: Pick one debut and find the shot its director has been remaking ever since.

films.title: 12 Angry Men
films.why: Lumet’s debut — one room, twelve men, total command of geography

films.title: Eraserhead
films.why: Lynch arrives fully formed — industrial dread as lullaby

films.title: Badlands
films.why: Malick’s debut — murder narrated like a storybook

films.title: Blood Simple
films.why: the Coens’ debut — noir mechanics, Texas heat

films.title: Get Out
films.why: Peele’s debut — horror as social thesis, timed like comedy

courses.title: The image — cinematography
courses.desc: Stop watching the story; watch the light. Where the camera sits, what the lens does, and how photography argues before anyone speaks.
courses.assignment: In The Conformist, note which direction the light comes from in every political scene.

films.title: The Conformist
films.why: Storaro — how light argues politics

films.title: Barry Lyndon
films.why: candlelight and NASA lenses; painting as photography

films.title: Roma
films.why: the 65mm memory — staging in depth, everything in focus

films.title: The Tree of Life
films.why: natural light chasing grace notes

films.title: Mirror
films.why: Tarkovsky — images as memory itself

courses.title: The cut — editing
courses.desc: The only art unique to cinema. Rhythm, ellipsis, and structure — the film that exists only in the splice.
courses.assignment: Raging Bull: pick one fight and map where the camera is allowed to be.

films.title: Raging Bull
films.why: the fights — every cut a punch

films.title: Whiplash
films.why: rhythm as violence; music cut like action

films.title: All That Jazz
films.why: editing as autobiography — cut by the man dying in it

films.title: Memento
films.why: structure as editing — the film only exists in the cut

films.title: Sherlock Jr.
films.why: Keaton walks into the screen — montage as magic

courses.title: Sound & music

extra.title: Berberian Sound Studio
extra.why: a giallo made of sound alone

courses.desc: Half the picture is invisible. Design, silence, and score — what the ear is told while the eye is busy.
courses.assignment: Watch the last ten minutes of The Conversation with your eyes closed. Then rewatch.

films.title: The Conversation
films.why: surveillance as sound design; the mix is the plot

films.title: Blow Out
films.why: a movie about a sound man, built like one

films.title: A Man Escaped
films.why: sound carries the escape; the image just waits

films.title: No Country for Old Men
films.why: the score that isn’t there

films.title: Sound of Metal
films.why: subjective sound — deafness mixed from the inside

courses.title: The screenplay
courses.desc: Structure, dialogue, and the load a single scene can carry. Read these with your ears.
courses.assignment: Network: pick the least famous speech and explain why it still had to be there.

films.title: Network
films.why: Chayefsky — rhetoric as drama; every speech load-bearing

films.title: Annie Hall
films.why: structure as confession

films.title: Before Sunset
films.why: real-time dialogue with an iceberg underneath

films.title: The Social Network
films.why: the deposition as engine

films.title: Withnail & I
films.why: quotable is not the same as good — this is both

courses.title: Performance & directing actors
courses.desc: What the camera does to acting, and what a director can ask of a human face at close range.
courses.assignment: A Woman Under the Influence: find the moment Rowlands stops performing for the other characters.

films.title: On the Waterfront
films.why: the Method arrives — the taxi scene

films.title: A Woman Under the Influence
films.why: Cassavetes and Rowlands — acting past the edge of safety

films.title: Scenes from a Marriage
films.why: two faces, six hours, no hiding

films.title: The Master
films.why: two performances circling like animals

films.title: Marriage Story
films.why: the argument scene — blocking as escalation

courses.title: German Expressionism & Weimar
courses.desc: The set as psyche, the shadow as character. Everything noir and horror borrowed starts here.
courses.assignment: Nosferatu: sketch the three most distorted frames. What is each distortion for?

films.title: The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari
films.why: cinema discovers style — the painted mind

films.title: Nosferatu
films.why: the shadow on the stairs; horror grammar, first edition

films.title: The Last Laugh
films.why: Murnau unchains the camera — no intertitles needed

films.title: Pandora’s Box
films.why: Louise Brooks — the modern face

films.title: The Blue Angel
films.why: Dietrich; sound arrives in Weimar, cruelty intact

courses.title: Melodrama — the Sirk line
courses.desc: The most underestimated register in cinema: feeling at full volume, critique smuggled in the décor.
courses.assignment: All That Heaven Allows: inventory what the furniture says that the dialogue can’t.

films.title: All That Heaven Allows
films.why: the widow and the gardener — critique dressed as kitsch

films.title: Ali: Fear Eats the Soul
films.why: Fassbinder remakes Sirk with a migrant worker — sharper knife

films.title: Brief Encounter
films.why: repression as romance; the train platform of the century

films.title: Far From Heaven
films.why: Haynes closes the loop — pastiche with a pulse

films.title: Mother India
films.why: the Indian melodrama at national-myth scale

courses.title: Latin American cinema

extra.title: The Secret in Their Eyes
extra.why: the stadium shot; genre with a wound

courses.desc: From revolutionary Cuba to the new Mexican and Brazilian waves — politics, velocity and the road.
courses.assignment: City of God: choose one edit that does sociology.

films.title: City of God
films.why: kinetic editing in service of sociology

films.title: Y Tu Mamá También
films.why: the road movie with a ghost narrator

films.title: Memories of Underdevelopment
films.why: alienation inside a revolution

films.title: Amores Perros
films.why: three stories, one crash — a national cinema announces itself

films.title: Central Station
films.why: the humanist road — Brazil at eye level

courses.title: Africa & the Middle East

extra.title: The Color of Pomegranates
extra.why: the tableau film — icons in motion

courses.desc: The cinemas most syllabi skip: Sembène’s declaration, the Senegalese wave, Kiarostami’s questions, and insurgency shot like newsreel.
courses.assignment: Taste of Cherry: decide what the ending does to everything before it. Change your mind once.

films.title: Black Girl
films.why: Sembène — African cinema’s declaration of independence

films.title: Touki Bouki
films.why: the Senegalese new wave — style to burn

films.title: Taste of Cherry
films.why: a car, a question, an ending that hands you the film

films.title: The Battle of Algiers
films.why: the insurgency manual, shot like newsreel

films.title: Timbuktu
films.why: occupation rendered in irony and grace

courses.title: Women behind the camera

extra.title: Meek’s Cutoff
extra.why: Reichardt — the western at walking pace

courses.desc: Not a genre — a correction. Five directors, five entirely different answers to what a film is for.
courses.assignment: Beau Travail: explain the final dance to someone who hasn’t seen the film. Fail. Watch it again.

films.title: The Piano
films.why: Campion — desire scored for mud and keys

films.title: Beau Travail
films.why: Denis — the body as text; that final dance

films.title: Lost in Translation
films.why: Coppola — mood as narrative

films.title: Vagabond
films.why: Varda — a death told backwards, without pity

films.title: The Hurt Locker
films.why: Bigelow — tension engineered frame by frame

courses.title: Queer cinema

extra.title: My Own Private Idaho
extra.why: River Phoenix; Shakespeare on the street

courses.desc: The gaze, reciprocated. Desire, performance and community — from the ballroom floor to the painted portrait.
courses.assignment: Portrait of a Lady on Fire: count who looks at whom, and when it becomes mutual.

films.title: Moonlight
films.why: three panels, one man — the close-up as tenderness

films.title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
films.why: the gaze, reciprocated — painting as courtship

films.title: Paris Is Burning
films.why: the ballroom documentary every syllabus owes

films.title: Happy Together
films.why: Wong — love as exile

films.title: Carol
films.why: glances across department stores — Haynes, sincere this time

courses.title: The avant-garde & the short film

extra.title: The Heart of the World
extra.why: Maddin — six minutes of pure mania

courses.desc: Cinema with the training wheels off: dream logic, collage, and sixteen-minute masterpieces. Where the medium tests itself.
courses.assignment: Meshes of the Afternoon runs fourteen minutes. Watch it three times in a row — it’s designed for that.

films.title: Un Chien Andalou
films.why: the eyeball — cinema’s dream logic, opening statement

films.title: Meshes of the Afternoon
films.why: Deren — the American avant-garde begins at home

films.title: Koyaanisqatsi
films.why: image and Glass — the wordless argument

films.title: Daisies
films.why: Czech anarchy — collage, food fights, a censor’s nightmare

films.title: World of Tomorrow
films.why: sixteen minutes of stick figures; more ideas than most features

courses.title: The Hitchcock seminar — suspense as form
courses.desc: One director, one seminar. Suspense is information management: who knows what, and when. The bomb under the table, taught by its inventor.
courses.assignment: Pick one scene and write down what the audience knows that the characters don’t. That gap is the engine.

films.title: Rear Window
films.why: voyeurism as film-watching — the point-of-view essay

films.title: Notorious
films.why: the crane shot to the key; suspense built from love and a wine cellar

films.title: Shadow of a Doubt
films.why: his own favourite — evil at the family table

films.title: Strangers on a Train
films.why: the transference plot; the tennis match and the carousel

films.title: North by Northwest
films.why: pure cinema — set pieces strung on a MacGuffin

semester.title: The grammar — silents & foundations
semester.desc: Where the language was invented: framing, montage, the close-up, and finally sound. Everything after is dialect.

next.title: Sunrise: A Song of Two Humans
next.why: pure visual storytelling — the camera untethered

card.title: Sunrise: A Song of Two Humans
card.why: pure visual storytelling — the camera untethered
